# 📘 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. Define Detailed Project Report (DPR) in the context of watershed development. [KTU University Exam — May 2024]

**Model Answer:**
A **Detailed Project Report (DPR)** is a comprehensive document that consolidates **baseline survey data, resource inventories, hydrological analysis, treatment plans, engineering designs, cost estimates, institutional arrangements, and socio-economic analysis** for a watershed project. As per the **DoLR / PMKSY-WDC guidelines**, the DPR is the mandatory technical and financial blueprint required for **sanction and funding** of watershed development projects by Central and State agencies. It typically follows a **standard 13-chapter format**.

*(3 marks — full marks for: definition + DoLR reference + 13-chapter mention)*

---

### Q2. List any four components of a DPR for watershed projects. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023]

**Model Answer:**
The four key components of a watershed DPR are:
1. **Baseline survey and resource inventory** (soil, slope, land use).
2. **Treatment plan / watershed treatment schedule** (area + drainage line treatments).
3. **Cost estimation and financial plan** (BOQ, funding pattern).
4. **Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) plan** with key indicators.

*(3 marks — any 4 valid components)*

---

## 5.2 Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks, with Internal Choice)

### 🅰️ QUESTION A — 14 Marks *(Attempt any one)*

**Q. (a)** Discuss in detail the **various components / chapters of a Detailed Project Report (DPR)** for a watershed development project as per DoLR / PMKSY-WDC guidelines. **(7 marks)**

**Q. (b)** Explain the **economic and financial analysis** carried out in a DPR. Derive the **BCR** and **NPV** equations and state the criteria for project acceptance. **(7 marks)**

---

### ✅ MODEL SOLUTION — Question A

#### Part (a) — 7 Marks — Components of DPR

The DoLR / PMKSY-WDC mandates a **13-chapter structure**:

1. **Executive Summary** — One-page project snapshot for senior officials.
2. **Introduction & Background** — Location, geographic coordinates, accessibility, history.
3. **Basic Data of the Watershed** — Area, villages, households, demography (Census data).
4. **Baseline Survey & Resource Inventory** — Soil (NBSS&LUP), slope, geology, hydrogeology, vegetation, land use (remote sensing based).
5. **Analysis of Existing Systems** — Present cropping, irrigation, livestock; gap analysis.
6. **Problem Identification** — PRA, focused group discussions, SWOT analysis.
7. **Treatment Plan** — Area treatment (contour bunds, terracing, afforestation) + Drainage line treatment (check dams, percolation tanks).
8. **Engineering Design & Cost Estimation** — Hydraulic design, drawings, BOQ, schedule of rates.
9. **Financial Plan & Funding Pattern** — Centre : State : Beneficiary ratio; convergence with MGNREGA.
10. **Economic & Social Analysis** — BCR, NPV, IRR, employment generation, gender impact.
11. **Institutional Arrangement** — WC, WCDC, PIA, SHGs, capacity building.
12. **Operation, Maintenance & Sustainability** — O&M corpus fund, community ownership.
13. **Monitoring, Evaluation & Learning** — Process, output, outcome indicators; MIS; third-party evaluation.

**Engineering rationale:** This cascading structure ensures **scientific diagnosis → planning → costing → appraisal → execution → sustainability**.

---

#### Part (b) — 7 Marks — Economic & Financial Analysis

**Definition:** Economic analysis evaluates whether the **benefits** of a watershed project justify its **costs** over its design life, using **time-value-of-money** principles.

**Key parameters:**

- **Project life (T):** Typically 20–25 years for plantations, 5–7 years for structures.
- **Discount rate (i):** NABARD uses 10%; World Bank projects use 8–12%.

**Derivation of BCR:**

$$
BCR = \dfrac{\sum_{t=0}^{T} \dfrac{B_t}{(1+i)^t}}{\sum_{t=0}^{T} \dfrac{C_t}{(1+i)^t}}
$$

Where $B_t$ = benefits in year $t$, $C_t$ = costs in year $t$, $i$ = discount rate.

**Decision rule:** $BCR > 1$ → **Accept**; $BCR = 1$ → Indifferent; $BCR < 1$ → **Reject**.

**Derivation of NPV:**

$$
NPV = \sum_{t=0}^{T} \dfrac{B_t - C_t}{(1+i)^t}
$$

**Decision rule:** $NPV \geq 0$ → **Accept**; $NPV < 0$ → **Reject**.

**Worked Example (Mini DPR):**

If a ₹5 lakh project yields discounted benefits of ₹5.45 lakh over 5 years at 10% discount:

$$
BCR = \dfrac{5.45}{5.00} = 1.09
$$

$$
NPV = 5.45 - 5.00 = \text{₹} 0.45 \; \text{lakh}
$$

Since BCR > 1 and NPV > 0, the project is **financially viable**.

**Practical implication:** NABARD / DoLR typically requires **BCR ≥ 1.5** for priority funding.

---

### 🅱️ QUESTION B — 14 Marks *(Alternative Choice)*

**Q. (a)** Describe the **step-by-step procedure for preparation of a DPR** for a watershed project, starting from reconnaissance survey to final sanction. **(7 marks)**

**Q. (b)** What is **cost estimation** in a watershed DPR? Explain the **cost escalation formula** with a numerical example. Also explain the **funding pattern under PMKSY-WDC**. **(7 marks)**

---

### ✅ MODEL SOLUTION — Question B

#### Part (a) — 7 Marks — DPR Preparation Procedure

The sequential procedure is:

1. **Reconnaissance Survey** — Preliminary visit, identification of watershed boundary, accessibility.
2. **Data Acquisition** — Toposheets (1:50,000), satellite data (Sentinel-2 / Landsat-8), IMD rainfall data, Census data.
3. **GIS & Remote Sensing Analysis** — Watershed delineation, drainage network, slope classification (DEM-based), LU/LC classification.
4. **Baseline Field Survey** — Soil sampling, vegetation inventory, hydrogeological assessment, socio-economic survey.
5. **PRA & Stakeholder Consultation** — Transect walks, focus group discussions, resource mapping.
6. **Problem Identification** — SWOT analysis, cause-effect diagrams.
7. **Treatment Plan Preparation** — Area treatment + drainage line treatment with activity-wise physical targets.
8. **Engineering Design & Cost Estimation** — Hydraulic structures, BOQ, schedule of rates.
9. **Economic Analysis** — BCR, NPV, IRR computation.
10. **DPR Compilation** — 13-chapter document preparation.
11. **Appraisal & Approval Chain** — Gram Sabha → WC → WCDC → DWDA → SLNA → DoLR.
12. **Fund Release & Implementation** — Disbursement, work execution.
13. **Monitoring & Evaluation** — Concurrent and third-party M&E.

---

#### Part (b) — 7 Marks — Cost Estimation & Funding Pattern

**Definition:** Cost estimation is the process of computing the **total financial requirement** of the project by activity-wise quantity × unit rate.

**Cost Escalation Formula:**

$$
C_f = C_p \left(1 + \dfrac{r}{100}\right)^n
$$

Where $C_f$ = future cost, $C_p$ = present cost, $r$ = annual escalation %, $n$ = years.

**Numerical Example:**

Present cost $C_p$ = ₹5,00,000; $r$ = 6%; $n$ = 3 years.

$$
C_f = 5{,}00{,}000 \times (1.06)^3 = 5{,}00{,}000 \times 1.191016
$$

$$
C_f = \text{₹} \; 5{,}95{,}508
$$

**Funding Pattern under PMKSY-WDC:**

| Category | Centre : State |
|---|---|
| Non-tribal / Plains | 60 : 40 |
| Tribal / Hilly / NE States | 90 : 10 |
| Convergence with MGNREGA | Labour component shared |
| Beneficiary contribution | 5–10% (community share) |

**Unit Cost Norm:** ₹12,000 – ₹15,000 / ha (PMKSY-WDC 2015 guideline).

---

## 5.3 ⚠️ KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common mistakes that cost 2–3 marks per question:**
>
> 1. **Confusing DPR with Project Proposal** — DPR is *detailed & technical*; a Project Proposal is a *short executive request*. Examiners deduct marks for interchange.
> 2. **Forgetting the appraisal hierarchy** — Students often write "DRDA approves" but the correct path is *Gram Sabha → WC → WCDC → DWDA → SLNA → DoLR*.
> 3. **Skipping numerical working in BCR/NPV** — Writing the formula alone gets only 2/7 marks. Always show **a numerical worked example**.
> 4. **Wrong funding ratio** — Writing 50:50 instead of **60:40 (plains) / 90:10 (NE-tribal)** is a guaranteed 1-mark loss.
> 5. **Not stating the discount rate** — A BCR without the corresponding $i$ value is meaningless.
> 6. **Omitting the convergence clause** — PMKSY-WDC mandates **MGNREGA convergence**; missing it = 0.5 mark lost.

---

## 5.4 🎯 Topic Recap — Rapid Revision Checklist

- ✅ **DPR** = Detailed, technical, financial & institutional blueprint of a watershed project.
- ✅ **13-chapter structure** is mandated by **DoLR / PMKSY-WDC**.
- ✅ DPR preparation follows: **Reconnaissance → Data → GIS mapping → PRA → Treatment Plan → Costing → Economic Analysis → Appraisal → Sanction → Implementation → M&E**.
- ✅ **Approval hierarchy:** Gram Sabha → WC → WCDC → DWDA → SLNA → DoLR (Gol).
- ✅ **Funding pattern:** 60:40 (plains), 90:10 (tribal/NE) under PMKSY-WDC.
- ✅ **Unit cost norm:** ₹12,000–15,000 per hectare (DoLR benchmark).
- ✅ **Key financial formulas:** Cost escalation, NPV, BCR, IRR.
- ✅ **BCR > 1** → Accept; **NPV ≥ 0** → Accept; **IRR > bank rate (10%)** → Accept.
- ✅ DPR integrates **GIS, remote sensing, PRA, hydrology, soil science, economics, and institutional design** — it is **truly multidisciplinary**.
- ✅ Project life: **5–7 years** for implementation + **20 years** for evaluation.
- ✅ **Convergence:** MGNREGA, NREGA, MNREGA labour components.
- ✅ Sustainability ensured via **O&M corpus fund** and **community ownership**.

---
